title: Tool Frontmatter
description: Schema for the YAML frontmatter in every <tools_dir>/<id>.md file
x-file-example:
---
id: pytorch
collection: tool
name: "PyTorch"
category: framework
url: https://pytorch.org
sources:
  - "https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch"
  - "https://pytorch.org/docs/"
languages:
  - name: "Python"
    code_percentage: 100
docker: true
cloud: false
platform: ["linux", "macos", "windows"]
license: "BSD-3-Clause"
version: "2.3.0"
last_checked: 2026-06-03
maturity: mature
install: "pip install torch"
alternatives:
  - "[[tensorflow]]"
  - "[[jax]]"
tags:
  - deep-learning
  - training
concepts:
  - "[[neural_networks]]"
  - "[[backprop]]"
verdict: recommended
---

## Description
Deep learning framework with dynamic computation graphs.

## Pros
- Pythonic API

## Cons

## Notes

id.description: Snake_case ID used as the filename and in [[wikilinks]]

collection.description: Node type — used by Obsidian-style apps to filter nodes by collection

name.description: Display name of the tool, library, or framework

url.description: Official homepage or docs URL. Must not duplicate any entry in 'sources'.

sources.description: All known URLs for this tool — repo (GitHub/GitLab), docs, blog, etc. The ingest URL lands here when it differs from 'url'. Must not contain the value of 'url'.

languages.description: Programming languages the tool is written in or targets, each with a name and code coverage percentage

code_percentage.description: Percentage of the codebase written in this language

docker.description: Whether an official Docker image or docker-compose setup is available

cloud.description: Whether a managed cloud / hosted service is available (e.g. Pinecone, Weaviate Cloud)

license.description: SPDX license identifier or 'proprietary' — relevant for production use decisions

version.description: Version recorded at time of last_checked — snapshot, not live

last_checked.description: ISO date when this node's metadata was last verified (YYYY-MM-DD) — tool info ages fast

install.description: One-line install command (e.g. 'pip install torch', 'npm install langchain')

alternatives.description: [[wikilink]] references to similar or competing tool nodes

concepts.description: [[wikilink]] references to curriculum concepts this tool relates to

verdict.description: User's recommendation — only set from explicit user opinion, never inferred
